frameworkComparisons.case: EX01 Battle of the Heavenly Titans
frameworkComparisons.prompt: Battle sequence with cross-shot identity and action continuity.

evidence.title: Identity splits into three Wukong-like figures
evidence.text: The duel no longer preserves the two original roles: the frame contains multiple monkey-like bodies and a tiny foreground figure.

evidence.title: Golden staff turns into the wrong weapon
evidence.text: The weapon state changes from the expected staff relation into a glowing vertical blade-like form.

evidence.title: Weapon direction no longer explains the hit
evidence.text: The oversized weapon points through the frame, but the attacker-target relation is hard to infer.

evidence.title: Three-person setup replaces the duel
evidence.text: The opening frame introduces an extra body and a different spacing pattern before the battle continuity is established.

evidence.title: Character identity changes completely
evidence.text: By 1:49 the figure, costume silhouette, and combat layout no longer match the starting battle state.

evidence.title: Anchored opening clash
evidence.text: Faces, locked weapons, and opposing body lines keep the continuation tied to the observed battle state.

evidence.title: Aerial combat remains readable
evidence.text: Both fighters and the staff lock stay in one clear action line as the camera moves into the sky battle.

evidence.title: Late story action pays off
evidence.text: The detection beat reads as a deliberate continuation of the duel rather than a disconnected spectacle shot.

frameworkComparisons.prompt: Boxing motion sequence with stable character pose, rhythm, and event causality.

evidence.title: Punching bag leaves a ghosted duplicate
evidence.text: The bag/action cue appears doubled, making the training hit visually unstable.

evidence.title: Boxer and bag stay in one setup
evidence.text: The athlete, heavy bag, timer, and gym framing remain aligned as a continuous training scene.

evidence.title: Close-up preserves the same fighter
evidence.text: The tighter shot changes camera distance without losing the boxer identity or the gym environment.

evidence.title: Reaction beat follows the workout
evidence.text: The later shot reads as a consequence of the training rather than a reset to a new scene.

frameworkComparisons.prompt: Food-making sequence requiring object state, hand motion, and scene continuity.

evidence.title: Sequence is over-extended
evidence.text: The generated video runs much longer than the compact continuation needed for this cooking example.

evidence.title: Hands, dough, and table stay connected
evidence.text: The close working surface keeps the dumpling process tied to the same kitchen activity.

evidence.title: Family table relation remains stable
evidence.text: The adults and child continue the same food-preparation scene with consistent table placement.

evidence.title: Later shot still shows process progress
evidence.text: The accumulated dumplings and hand motion make the sequence feel like continued preparation.

frameworkComparisons.prompt: World landmark sequence requiring long-range scene handoff and coherent visual progression.

evidence.title: Landmark fades into a ghosted overlay
evidence.text: The cliffs and water blend with a transparent building image, making the destination handoff look unresolved.

evidence.title: City appears as a hard reset
evidence.text: The skyline at night arrives without a visible spatial bridge from the previous landscape.

evidence.title: Bridge view lacks transition context
evidence.text: The harbor landmark is visible, but the sequence does not preserve a clear route from the earlier location.

evidence.title: Atmosphere hides the landmark handoff
evidence.text: The distant skyline is heavily softened by haze, so the transition reads more like a mood shot than a clear location continuation.

evidence.title: Landscape reset without geographic bridge
evidence.text: The fjord-like view is coherent by itself, but it does not carry visible cues from the previous landmark.

evidence.title: Night skyline changes the visual thread
evidence.text: The lighting and city identity shift sharply, weakening the sense of one planned landmark tour.

evidence.title: Iconic structure appears as a standalone cut
evidence.text: The building is recognizable, but the frame does not explain how it follows from the previous geography.

evidence.title: Spatial context resets again
evidence.text: The harbor bridge shot is clean, but it feels inserted rather than connected to the earlier landmark path.

evidence.title: Late view drifts into generic skyline
evidence.text: The final city-water frame loses the specific landmark progression established earlier.

evidence.title: Opera House and harbor stay explicit
evidence.text: The Sydney Opera House, bridge, and waterline remain clear in the same wide establishing view.

evidence.title: Location shift keeps water and skyline cues
evidence.text: The camera changes viewpoint while keeping the harbor structure and city context readable.

evidence.title: Late shot preserves landmark identity
evidence.text: The bridge, skyline, and water reflections stay tied to the same destination instead of becoming a generic city shot.
